seo_title: GRASP: A Deterministic Framework for Ranking Arguments in…
meta_description: A new framework proposed to address inter-model disagreement in holistic judging of large language models, offering a transparent and auditable alternative.
body_html:
GRASP proposes a deterministic framework to address inter-model disagreement in holistic judging of large language models.

Key Benefits

  • Offers a transparent and auditable alternative to holistic LLM judging
  • Improves consistency and reproducibility of global rankings
  • Separates argumentative structure from rhetorical appeal

Technical Details

Key Concept Description
Convergent Attack-Defense Propagation Operator A novel operator that aggregates stable local interaction judgments into a global ranking
Structural Sufficiency A defense-aware notion of argument robustness over the explicit interaction graph

Limitations and Future Work

GRASP scores do not correlate with human 'convincingness' labels, highlighting a vital sociotechnical distinction.

Further research is needed to explore the potential applications of GRASP in various domains.

source.title: GRASP: Deterministic argument ranking in interaction graphs
source.summary: Researchers have found that large language models used as automated judges often produce inconsistent results when evaluating arguments, leading to inter-model disagreement. To address this issue, they propose a new framework called GRASP (Gradual Ranking with Attacks and Support Propagation), which aggregates local interaction judgments into a global ranking using a convergent attack-defense propagation operator. This approach produces more consistent rankings than holistic judging methods, without relying on subjective measures of persuasiveness or factuality.
